cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The task is to figure out how the author introduced the topic of the article. The question is asking about the method the author used to introduce the topic. The passage starts with a series of questions: "Do you start your work by making a list... Do you always take a list... Do you write down all the steps..." These are questions directed at the reader. Then, after the questions, the author says if you're a natural list maker, this habit is a good thing, and then proceeds to list several reasons why (gives memory a kick, helps stay in the moment, relieves stress, helps planning, prioritizes, boosts happiness). The options are: A) asking a series of questions B) presenting some simple facts C) stating his own point of view D) listing the benefits of a to-do list The introduction is the beginning part where the topic is introduced. The first few sentences are questions, so the author is using questions to engage the reader and introduce the topic. Then the actual discussion of benefits comes after that. So the introduction method is through asking questions, which is option A. Let me double-check. The question is about how the author introduces the topic, not what the topic is about. The topic is about the benefits of making to-do lists. The introduction method is the initial part, which is asking questions. The rest of the passage lists the benefits (option D), but that's
